title: Cross-Border Stablecoin Register — obligation record
description: One verified (jurisdiction x instrument x dimension) regulatory fact, versioned and sourced. v0.4.0: focus set expanded to twelve jurisdictions (US, EU, UK, SG, HK, CN, BR, CH, AE, TW, JP, KR), aligned to the maintainer's revised substrate — the Cross-Border Stablecoin Architecture v3 (Twelve Jurisdictions, June 2026) eight-constraint framework and the Multi-Jurisdiction Compliance Matrix v0.9.6. v0.7.0: adds the required `claim_class` axis (tier1_legal vs tier2_operational), orthogonal to `evidence_tier` (provenance strength) and `status` (in force vs draft), enabling the lawyer-citable subset = tier1_legal AND in_force AND resolution_text. v0.9.5 adds the orthogonal `binding_status` axis (binding status of the cited instrument), which caps citability: resolution_text is permitted only where binding_status is in_force_enacted.

dimension.description: 15-value set. Split of the former reserve_capital into reserve_backing + capital_requirements, and four dimensions added from the eight-constraint framework: securities_classification (C4), bank_nonbank_routing (C5), monetary_sovereignty (C7), disclosure_reporting (C8).

constraint_ref.description: Optional link to the eight-constraint framework, e.g. 'C3' or 'C4xC7'.

operational_notes.description: Tier-2 operational/market illustrations attached to this record (a product launch, a registration, a working example), kept OUT of the load-bearing legal fields so they are never projected as citable law. This is the per-record home for a market fact that contextualises a tier1_legal claim: the claim itself stays in requirement_summary/source (citable to the instrument), while 'USDC went live via SBI VC Trade on 2025-03-26' lives here, read as-of-dated. The citable projection (citable_subset / citable_law) deliberately omits this field; the build forbids the same operational material from appearing in a citable record's source.primary or pinpoint.

interpretive_flag.description: Carries the Compliance Matrix's [interpretive question] flag: the open tension and its expected resolution channel.

claim_class.description: The EPISTEMIC KIND of the record's load-bearing claim, distinct from how well it is sourced (evidence_tier) and from whether it is in force (status). 'tier1_legal' = a proposition of law: what a binding instrument (statute, regulation, official guidance) requires, permits, or prohibits — a fact a lawyer could cite to the instrument. 'tier2_operational' = a report of market/operational fact: what is actually live, who is registered, what products launched, what banking rails or liquidity exist — corroborated by registrations, company filings, market data, or media, and properly read as-of-dated. The distinction follows the Corridor Atlas §7 split between primary-source legal constraints (Tier 1) and market-reported operability (Tier 2), which the Atlas warns must not be read at the same confidence. The lawyer-citable subset is the intersection claim_class=tier1_legal AND status=in_force AND evidence_tier=resolution_text. A draft legal provision is still tier1_legal (its non-bindingness is carried by status=proposed), and an operational fact is tier2_operational even when confirmed against an official registration page (its kind, not its sourcing, is what excludes it from citable law).

evidence_tier.description: Provenance of the load-bearing facts. resolution_text = confirmed against the official normativo/statutory text; firm_summary = corroborated by law-firm/practitioner analysis but not yet against the official text; mixed = some of each. Independent of confidence, which measures corroboration strength.

binding_status.description: The BINDING STATUS of the instrument the cell rests on — a third axis, orthogonal to evidence_tier (how well-sourced) and claim_class (legal vs operational). It caps citability independently of verification: a cell can only be cited as CURRENT LAW (resolution_text) if its instrument is actually enacted-and-in-force. 'in_force_enacted' = an enacted statute/regulation in force now (the only status eligible for resolution_text). 'made_not_commenced' = made/passed but not yet commenced (e.g. a statutory instrument with a future commencement day) — real law, not yet operative. 'finalized_policy_pending' = a finalized regulator policy still awaiting implementing legislation (not enacted law). 'pending_proposal' = a pending bill, NPRM, or consultation (not law). 'prohibition' = the cell rests on a prohibition/absence-of-regime, so there is no positive permitted-activity rule to confirm (cannot be resolution_text as a positive proposition). 'no_regime' = no operative regime addresses the cell. The build forbids resolution_text on any status other than in_force_enacted.

verification.description: Audit record of HOW the load-bearing fact was verified, supporting a safe, traceable evidence_tier promotion. method=official_text means the claim was confirmed against the primary statutory/normative text (the basis for resolution_text/mixed); method=practitioner_corroboration means it rests on law-firm/practitioner analysis (the basis for firm_summary). 'against' identifies what it was checked against; 'excerpt' is an OPTIONAL short operative phrase (keep minimal — provenance, not reproduction).
